title: Replace regex handoff scraping with a structured MCP tool call
source: Anthropic docs: Tool use & structured outputs — https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/tool-use ; MCP tools
description: Inter-Morty handoffs are extracted by regex-scraping stdout for a <handoff_json>...</handoff_json> block (_extract_handoff_json, delegate.py:493-500). This is fragile: malformed/missing fences, truncation under streaming, or the model wrapping the block in prose all silently drop the handoff (JSONDecodeError returns None). The orchestrator already attaches an MCP server (delegate.py:1793-1805), so a dedicated tool with a validated schema removes the parsing layer entirely and guarantees well-formed, typed handoffs.
proposed_changes: Add an 'emit_handoff' MCP tool in mcp_server.py whose input schema mirrors the handoff envelope (to, content, type, interface, artifacts) and writes directly to the team shared-context path used at delegate.py:624-650. Instruct Morty's in the system prompt to call emit_handoff instead of printing a <handoff_json> block. Keep _extract_handoff_json as a fallback for one release, but prefer the tool-recorded handoff when present so structured/validated data wins over scraped text.
